paragraph: The single-family market in Norwood is competitive but not frenzied, with 26 homes sold over the last six months at a median of $928,500. Sellers are seeing modest success getting their asking prices, with about 46 percent of homes selling above list and a sale-to-list ratio right around 1.0. Median days on market sits at 20, which means priced homes are moving, but there is no urgency pushing buyers to waive inspections or overbid. The range is wide, from $600,000 on the low end to nearly $3.8 million at the top, so the market is genuinely stratified by neighborhood and condition. Condos and townhouses are lighter on volume with 7 sales at a median of $800,000, tracking nearly at asking price with a 28 percent sell-above rate and an 18-day median time on market. The buyer considering Norwood right now has leverage to negotiate, especially if the home needs work or sits in a less competitive pocket. You're not racing against multiple offers on most properties, and sellers know it.
